user_prompt: Describe Bernoulli’s principle through an airplane wing example in depth.

style_bible.visual_style: Clean educational CGI with realistic airplane wing, airflow streamlines, and subtle graphic overlays. Neutral colors with high contrast for text.
style_bible.camera_rules: Prioritize clarity over spectacle. Limited motion. Explanatory moments must be static holds.
style_bible.lighting_rules: Soft daylight with even exposure, no harsh shadows, wing surface clearly readable.
style_bible.continuity_rules: Airplane wing orientation, airflow direction, and background sky must remain consistent across shots and clips.

clips.clip_script: Introduce the airplane wing and visualize airflow splitting above and below as the plane moves forward.

shots.framing: Wide exterior view of airplane wing against blue sky
shots.subject_action: Airplane wing cruises steadily; faint airflow lines begin to appear
shots.continuity_note: Airplane maintains level flight; airflow direction established for next shot.
shots.lighting: Natural daylight evenly illuminating wing surface
shots.cinematic_look: Crisp, realistic CGI with educational clarity

shots.framing: Medium close-up of wing cross-section from the side
shots.subject_action: Airflow lines split at wing leading edge, moving faster over top surface
shots.continuity_note: Airflow speed difference clearly visible; prepares for pressure explanation.

shots.framing: Angled close-up along top surface of wing
shots.subject_action: Fast-moving airflow lines sweep smoothly over curved wing surface
shots.continuity_note: FINAL FRAME HANDOFF: Camera settles with wing angled left-to-right, airflow continuing steadily for clip 2 start.

clips.negative_prompt: logos, watermarks, brand marks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit, unreadable tiny text

clips.clip_script: Build on airflow by explaining pressure differences and how they generate lift.

shots.framing: Continuation close-up of wing from same angle as previous final frame
shots.subject_action: Airflow continues; subtle color shading begins to indicate pressure zones
shots.continuity_note: Maintains identical wing orientation and airflow from clip 1 final frame.

shots.framing: Side view of wing with clear pressure visualization above and below
shots.subject_action: Low-pressure zone above wing shown in cool colors; higher pressure below in warm colors
shots.continuity_note: Pressure contrast clearly established; leads to lift visualization.
shots.lighting: Neutral lighting to keep colors accurate

shots.framing: Wider view showing entire wing with upward force arrows
shots.subject_action: Arrows illustrate upward lift force acting on wing as plane maintains altitude
shots.continuity_note: FINAL FRAME HANDOFF: Wing centered with visible lift arrows frozen at end for clear takeaway.

clips.negative_prompt: logos, watermarks, brand marks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit, unreadable tiny text
clips.camera: External stabilized camera aligned to wing

full_script_20s: A commercial airplane wing in flight is shown from outside. Airflow lines reveal how air moves over and under the wing. The video visually explains that faster airflow above the wing creates lower pressure compared to below, producing lift. The explanation deepens by showing pressure difference forces pushing the wing upward, maintaining continuity across both clips.
